title: Aerospace Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can accelerate aerodynamic CFD sweeps and structural sizing iterations, but flight-certification analysis and safety-margin decisions are tightly regulated and engineer-owned.
rationales.toolMaturity: CFD and structural-optimization tools with AI-assisted surrogate modeling are improving fast but remain specialist, not commodity, tooling.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated at major primes and a handful of well-funded startups; most of the supply chain still relies on traditional analysis cycles.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run overnight CFD and FEA batch sweeps and surface anomalies, but certification documentation and airworthiness sign-off stay with licensed engineers.

tasksGrowing: Novel propulsion and materials system integration
Failure investigation on physical test articles
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2011, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Design-iteration cycles are compressing as AI-assisted CFD and structural tools mature, but certification and airworthiness sign-off remain tightly regulated, engineer-owned work that AI cannot touch.
